capability_objective: From the long expense fixture, resolve “Intercity Rail Fare” in Chicago, status active. Start with a scoped search; do not guess an ID from the surrounding records. After search identifies the record, fetch its full details by that returned ID. Ignore same-name records in other locations and slightly different names in Chicago. Make no persistent changes. Finish with one sentence beginning “Found:” and include the stable ID.
prompt:
Resolve one expense in the provided sandbox. Read README.md first, then use the genuine local `./expensectl` executable only through Pi's Bash tool. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing data, generated runtime data, or execution evidence, and do not inspect, parse, modify, or execute anything under `.harness`.

Locate the exact expense named “Intercity Rail Fare” in the exact location “Chicago” and confirm that its complete current record has status `active`. Begin with a scoped exact-name-and-location search. The search is discovery only: do not report from its summary, infer an ID from surrounding records, or use an ID supplied anywhere except that search's returned match. Advance only if the search returns exactly one match with one nonempty stable ID. After the search Bash call has returned, make a separate Bash call to retrieve the complete record using exactly that returned stable ID. Do not combine the search and retrieval in one Bash call. If the search is missing, ambiguous, or malformed, stop without retrieving or guessing and explain the unresolved lookup instead of creating the deliverable.

This is a read-only lookup. Invoke no ledger operation except the one required search and the one eligible retrieval. In particular, do not list records, inspect a profile, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not alter the ledger, its backing or runtime data, its audit evidence, or protected verification files; do not manually create, edit, replay, or delete execution evidence. Ignore same-name expenses in other locations, slightly different names in Chicago, stale entries, and every unrelated record. Leave no scratch artifacts.

After the complete record is retrieved, write `finding.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly one newline-terminated sentence in this form: `Found: <name> in <location> has status <status> (stable ID <stable_id>).` Replace every placeholder only with the corresponding value from the complete record, preserving it exactly. Add no heading, bullets, blank lines, date, amount, explanation, or extra prose. Return exactly the same sentence as the final response.
